notes_md:
**ngon** means *delicious* or *tasty*. You use it to say food or drinks taste really good.

It’s an adjective, so it comes after **rất / quá / lắm** to make it stronger. It describes food, drinks, and sometimes smells.

Very common opposite: **dở** (not tasty, bad). A stronger or more expressive version is **ngon lắm** (very delicious).
exampleSentences: Món phở này thật sự rất ngon, em muốn ăn thêm một tô nữa.
Bánh mì chị làm sáng nay ngon quá, mọi người đều khen.
Anh thấy trái cây ở chợ này rất tươi và ngon.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This pho is really delicious, I want another bowl.
The bread you made this morning is delicious, everyone praised it.
I think the fruit at this market is very fresh and delicious.

definitions.vi: Có vị rất ngon; thơm ngon.

notes:
ngon means delicious or tasty. You use it to say food or drinks taste really good.

It’s an adjective, so it comes after rất / quá / lắm to make it stronger. It describes food, drinks, and sometimes smells.

Very common opposite: dở (not tasty, bad). A stronger or more expressive version is ngon lắm (very delicious).
